devanagari: पुरुवंशवर्णनं तत्र दुष्यंतभरतयोश्चरितम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । पूरोर्वंशं प्रवक्ष्यामि यत्र जातोऽसि भारत । यत्र राजर्षयो वंश्या ब्रह्मवंश्याश्...
english_devnagari: puruvaṃśavarṇanaṃ tatra duṣyaṃtabharatayoścaritam śrīśuka uvāca pūrorvaṃśaṃ pravakṣyāmi yatra jāto yatra rājarṣayo vaṃśyā brahmavaṃśyāśca jajñire

[nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
I shall now narrate to you the history of Pūru’s line wherein you are born, Oh descendant of Bharata. In this family sprang up many royal sages and Brāhmaṇas who propagated their own lines.

devanagari: जनमेजयो ह्यभूत् पूरोः प्रचिन्वांस्तत् सुतस्ततः । प्रवीरोऽथ मनुस्युर्वै तस्माच्चारुपदोऽभवत् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: janamejayo hyabhūt pūroḥ pracinvāṃstat sutastataḥ pravīro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Janamejaya was born of Pūru; Janamejaya’s son was Pracinvān. His son was Pravīra who begot Namasyu. To him was born Cārupada.

devanagari: तस्य सुद्युरभूत् पुत्रः तस्माद् बहुगवस्ततः । संयातिस्तस्याहंयाती रौद्राश्वस्तत्सुतः स्मृतः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya sudyurabhūt putraḥ tasmād bahugavastataḥ saṃyātistasyāhaṃyātī raudrāśvastatsutaḥ smṛtaḥ
translation: Sudyu sprang from Cārupada; Sudyu had a son Bahugava; thence was born Saṃyāti; his son was Ahaṃyāti whose son was Raudrāśva.

devanagari: ऋतेयुस्तस्य कक्षेयुः स्थण्डिलेयुः कृतेयुकः । जलेयुः सन्नतेयुश्च धर्मसत्यव्रतेयवः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛteyustasya kakṣeyuḥ sthaṇḍileyuḥ kṛteyukaḥ jaleyuḥ sannateyuśca dharmasatyavrateyavaḥ
translation: Raudrāśva begot on a celestial nymph Ghṛtāci ten sons viz. Ṛteyu, Kukṣeyu, Sthaṇḍileyu, Kṛteyu, Jaleyu, Santateyu, Dharmeyu, Satyeyu, Vrateyu and the youngest known as Vaneyu—even as ten sense organs (both conative and cognitive) are evolved from the chief vital air, the Soul of the world.

devanagari: दशैतेऽप्सरसः पुत्रा वनेयुश्चावमः स्मृतः । घृताच्यामिन्द्रियाणीव मुख्यस्य जगदात्मनः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: daśaite ghṛtācyāmindriyāṇīva mukhyasya jagadātmanaḥ
translation: Raudrāśva begot on a celestial nymph Ghṛtāci ten sons viz. Ṛteyu, Kukṣeyu, Sthaṇḍileyu, Kṛteyu, Jaleyu, Santateyu, Dharmeyu, Satyeyu, Vrateyu and the youngest known as Vaneyu—even as ten sense organs (both conative and cognitive) are evolved from the chief vital air, the Soul of the world.

devanagari: ऋतेयो रन्तिभारोऽभूत् त्रयस्तस्यात्मजा नृप । सुमतिर्ध्रुवोऽप्रतिरथः कण्वोऽप्रतिरथात्मजः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛteyo rantibhāro sumatirdhruvo
translation: From Ṛteyu was born Rantibhāra. He had the following three sons viz. Sumati, Dhruva and Apratiratha. Kaṇva was the son of Apratiratha, Oh King!

devanagari: तस्य मेधातिथिः तस्मात् प्रस्कण्वाद्या द्विजातयः । पुत्रोऽभूत् सुमते रेभ्यो दुष्यन्तः तत्सुतो मतः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya medhātithiḥ tasmāt praskaṇvādyā dvijātayaḥ putro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Kaṇva’s son was Medhātithi. From him were originated Praskaṇva and other Brāhmaṇa clans. Sumati had a son called Raibhya, and Duṣyanta was regarded as his son.

devanagari: दुष्यन्तो मृगयां यातः कण्वाश्रमपदं गतः । तत्रासीनां स्वप्रभया मण्डयन्तीं रमामिव ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: duṣyanto mṛgayāṃ yātaḥ kaṇvāśramapadaṃ gataḥ tatrāsīnāṃ svaprabhayā maṇḍayantīṃ ramāmiva
translation: While on a hunting excursion, (king) Duṣyanta happened to visit the hermitage of Kaṇva. Having seen a beautiful damsel beautifying the surroundings with her effulgence, and enchanting like the Māyā of the Lord, he instantaneously got infatuated. And though surrounded by some of his soldiers, he spoke to that charming lady.

devanagari: विलोक्य सद्यो मुमुहे देवमायामिव स्त्रियम् । बभाषे तां वरारोहां भटैः कतिपयैर्वृतः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vilokya sadyo mumuhe devamāyāmiva striyam babhāṣe tāṃ varārohāṃ bhaṭaiḥ katipayairvṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: While on a hunting excursion, (king) Duṣyanta happened to visit the hermitage of Kaṇva. Having seen a beautiful damsel beautifying the surroundings with her effulgence, and enchanting like the Māyā of the Lord, he instantaneously got infatuated. And though surrounded by some of his soldiers, he spoke to that charming lady.

devanagari: तद्दर्शनप्रमुदितः संनिवृत्तपरिश्रमः । पप्रच्छ कामसन्तप्तः प्रहसन् श्लक्ष्णया गिरा ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: taddarśanapramuditaḥ saṃnivṛttapariśramaḥ papraccha kāmasantaptaḥ prahasan ślakṣṇayā girā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Overjoyed at her very sight, he felt all his fatigue relieved. Tormented by sexual passion, he smilingly asked her, in soft words.

devanagari: का त्वं कमलपत्राक्षि कस्यासि हृदयंगमे । किं वा चिकीर्षितं त्वत्र भवत्या निर्जने वने ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: kā tvaṃ kamalapatrākṣi kasyāsi hṛdayaṃgame kiṃ vā cikīrṣitaṃ tvatra bhavatyā nirjane vane

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ ], [accusative single from √ kṛ ]

translation: “Who are you, Oh beautiful lady (with eyes like lotus-petals)? Whose daughter are you, Oh charming damsel? what is your object in staying in a tenantless forest?

devanagari: व्यक्तं राजन्यतनयां वेद्म्यहं त्वां सुमध्यमे । न हि चेतः पौरवाणां अधर्मे रमते क्वचित् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: vyaktaṃ rājanyatanayāṃ vedmyahaṃ tvāṃ sumadhyame na hi cetaḥ pauravāṇāṃ adharme ramate kvacit
translation: Oh lady of a beautiful waist! As I perceive it, you are obviously a girl from a royal family. For the mind of the descendants of Pūru, hardly finds delight in unrighteous paths.”

devanagari: श्रीशकुन्तलोवाच । विश्वामित्रात्मजैवाहं त्यक्ता मेनकया वने । वेदैतद् भगवान् कण्वो वीर किं करवाम ते ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśakuntalovāca viśvāmitrātmajaivāhaṃ tyaktā menakayā vane vedaitad bhagavān kaṇvo vīra kiṃ karavāma te

translation:
Śakuntalā replied:
“As the venerable sage Kaṇva knows it, I am the daughter of Viśvāmitra, abandoned by my mother Menakā in the forest. What should we do for you, Oh warrior?

devanagari: आस्यतां ह्यरविन्दाक्ष गृह्यतां अर्हणं च नः । भुज्यतां सन्ति नीवारा उष्यतां यदि रोचते ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: āsyatāṃ hyaravindākṣa gṛhyatāṃ arhaṇaṃ ca naḥ bhujyatāṃ santi nīvārā uṣyatāṃ yadi rocate
translation: Be pleased to take a seat, Oh lotus-eyed one, and accept our hospitality. Enjoy the boiled rice of wild paddy. You may stay here, if it pleases you.”

devanagari: दुष्यन्त उवाच । उपपन्नमिदं सुभ्रु जातायाः कुशिकान्वये । स्वयं हि वृणुते राज्ञां कन्यकाः सदृशं वरम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: duṣyanta uvāca upapannamidaṃ subhru jātāyāḥ kuśikānvaye svayaṃ hi vṛṇute rājñāṃ kanyakāḥ sadṛśaṃ varam

translation:
Duṣyanta said:
“Oh lady of beautiful eyebrows! Born as you are in the line of Kuśika, all this (hospitality) is quite proper and natural. Maidens of royal families select of their own accord, their life-mates”.

devanagari: ओमित्युक्ते यथाधर्मं उपयेमे शकुन्तलाम् । गान्धर्वविधिना राजा देशकालविधानवित् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: omityukte yathādharmaṃ upayeme śakuntalām gāndharvavidhinā rājā deśakālavidhānavit
translation: When she consented to it, he married Śakuntalā duly according to Gāndharva custom prescribed (for Kṣatriyas) in Dharmaśāstra, as the king was expert in the knowledge of what should be done at particular time and place according to Dharma.

devanagari: अमोघवीर्यो राजर्षिः महिष्यां वीर्यमादधे । श्वोभूते स्वपुरं यातः कालेनासूत सा सुतम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: amoghavīryo rājarṣiḥ mahiṣyāṃ vīryamādadhe śvobhūte svapuraṃ yātaḥ kālenāsūta sā sutam

translation: The royal sage of unfailing procreative capacity, deposited his semen in his queen. Next morning he returned to his capital. In due course, she was delivered of a son.

devanagari: कण्वः कुमारस्य वने चक्रे समुचिताः क्रियाः । बद्ध्वा मृगेन्द्रान् तरसा क्रीडति स्म स बालकः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṇvaḥ kumārasya vane cakre samucitāḥ kriyāḥ baddhvā mṛgendrān tarasā krīḍati sma sa bālakaḥ

translation: Kaṇva carried out the appropriate religious rites of the boy, in the forest. With his tremendous strength, the child used to catch hold of lions and played with them.

devanagari: तं दुरत्ययविक्रान्तं आदाय प्रमदोत्तमा । हरेः अंशांशसंभूतं भर्तुरन्तिकमागमत् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ duratyayavikrāntaṃ ādāya pramadottamā hareḥ aṃśāṃśasaṃbhūtaṃ bharturantikamāgamat

translation: Taking with her, her son who was born of a portion of Lord Hari, and was of invincible prowess, the excellent lady sought the audience of her royal consort.

devanagari: यदा न जगृहे राजा भार्यापुत्रावनिन्दितौ । श्रृण्वतां सर्वभूतानां खे वाग् आह अशरीरिणी ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yadā na jagṛhe rājā bhāryāputrāvaninditau śrṛṇvatāṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ khe vāg āha aśarīriṇī
translation: When the king did not accept his wife and child, though both of them were blameless and irreproachable, an incorporal voice from heaven announced, while all beings were listening.

devanagari: माता भस्त्रा पितुः पुत्रो येन जातः स एव सः । भरस्व पुत्रं दुष्यन्त मावमंस्थाः शकुन्तलाम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: mātā bhastrā pituḥ putro yena jātaḥ sa eva saḥ bharasva putraṃ duṣyanta māvamaṃsthāḥ śakuntalām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: “The mother is simply a receptacle. The son belongs to his father alone, of whom he is born. Nay, the father is himself (no other than) the son. Therefore, Oh Duṣyanta, bring up your son. Do not neglect Śakuntalā.

devanagari: रेतोधाः पुत्रो नयति नरदेव यमक्षयात् । त्वं चास्य धाता गर्भस्य सत्यमाह शकुन्तला ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: retodhāḥ putro nayati naradeva yamakṣayāt tvaṃ cāsya dhātā garbhasya satyamāha śakuntalā
translation:
Oh King, a son who propagates the line of the family takes away his father out of the abode of Yama.[1] You have deposited Bharata in the foetus (i.e. you are the real father). What Śakuntalā stated is the truth.[2]
22(A). A. Bring up your son Bharata who is imbued with a ray of Vāsudeva. He is friendly towards Brāhmins, true to his promise, capable of knowing and appreciating virtues and excellences and enhancing your reputation.
22(B). He is possessed of superior energy, is generous, grateful and ready to wait upon the elders.” Hearing this incorporal voice from the heaven, the king accepted his son.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
v.l.: In case of dispute between the father and the mother both claiming the child, the father's claim is upheld by Yama (Dharmaśāstra).

Footnote [2]:
Of. Mahābhārata Ādi. 74. 110-111. is verbatim the same.
bhastrā mātā pituḥ putro yena jātaḥ sa eva saḥ /
bharasva putraṃ duṣyanta māvamaṃsthāḥ śakuntalām //
retodhāḥ putra unnayati naradeva yamakṣayāt /

devanagari: पितरि उपरते सोऽपि चक्रवर्ती महायशाः । महिमा गीयते तस्य हरेरंशभुवो भुवि ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: pitari uparate so mahimā gīyate tasya hareraṃśabhuvo bhuvi
translation: When the father (Duṣyanta) expired, he (Bharata) became an emperor of great renown. The great glory of Bharata who was born from the ray of Lord Hari, is still eulogised on the earth.

devanagari: चक्रं दक्षिणहस्तेऽस्य पद्मकोशोऽस्य पादयोः । ईजे महाभिषेकेण सोऽभिषिक्तोऽधिराड् विभुः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: cakraṃ dakṣiṇahaste īje mahābhiṣekeṇa so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: He had the sign of discus on right palm and that of a lotus on the soles of his feet. That powerful king, the sole emperor of the earth was consecrated (on the throne) by the performance of Mahābhiṣeka. He performed many sacrifices.

devanagari: पञ्चपञ्चाशता मेध्यैः गंगायामनु वाजिभिः । मामतेयं पुरोधाय यमुनामनु च प्रभुः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: pañcapañcāśatā medhyaiḥ gaṃgāyāmanu vājibhiḥ māmateyaṃ purodhāya yamunāmanu ca prabhuḥ
translation: With fifty-five sacrificial horses he performed (horse) sacrifices along the course of the Gaṅgā (from its source to the sea) with Dīrgha-tamas (the son of Mamatā) as his sacrificial priest.

devanagari: अष्टसप्ततिमेध्याश्वान् बबन्ध प्रददद् वसु । भरतस्य हि दौष्यन्तेः अग्निः साचीगुणे चितः । सहस्रं बद्वशो यस्मिन् ब्राह्मणा गा विभेजिरे ॥ २६...
english_devnagari: aṣṭasaptatimedhyāśvān babandha pradadad vasu bharatasya hi dauṣyanteḥ agniḥ sācīguṇe citaḥ sahasraṃ badvaśo yasmin brāhmaṇā gā vibhejire

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Along the course of the Yamunā, he tied seventy- eight sacrificial horses (i. e. performed seventyeight horse-sacrifices on the bank of the river as it flows to join the Gaṅgā), liberally distributing wealth (among the Brāhmaṇas). The sacred fire of Bharata, the son of Duṣyanta was set up on an exceedingly excellent place where the thousand Brāhmaṇas (engaged in the sacrifice) divided among themselves cows (gifted by Bharata), each receiving his share of thirteen thousand eighty-four (Badva[3]) cows.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes the definition of badva:
iyaṃ ca badva-saṅkhyā ślokena saṅgṛhyate:
catur-daśānāṃ lakṣāṇāṃ saptādhika-śatāṃśakaḥ /
badvaṃ caturaśītyagra-sahasrāṇi trayodaśa //

devanagari: त्रयस्त्रिंशच्छतं ह्यश्वान् बद्ध्वा विस्मापयन् नृपान् । दौष्यन्तिरत्यगान्मायां देवानां गुरुमाययौ ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: trayastriṃśacchataṃ hyaśvān baddhvā vismāpayan nṛpān dauṣyantiratyagānmāyāṃ devānāṃ gurumāyayau

translation: Having thus tied to the sacrificial post one hundred and thirtythree horses, he sprang a surprise on the earthly kings. The son of Duṣyanta surpassed gods with his affluence, and he became one with Viṣṇu who is adorable to gods.

devanagari: मृगान् शुक्लदतः कृष्णान् हिरण्येन परीवृतान् । अदात्कर्मणि मष्णारे नियुतानि चतुर्दश ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛgān śukladataḥ kṛṣṇān hiraṇyena parīvṛtān adātkarmaṇi maṣṇāre niyutāni caturdaśa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: At the conclusion of the sacrificial ritual maṣṇāra[4] (or at a sacred place called Maṣṇāra), he donated as gift, fourteen lakhs of elephants of the mṛga type[5] black in colour, with their white tusk plated with gold.[6]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
No commentator from Bhāvāratha Dīpikā to Bhaktamanorañjanī is sure of the exact significance of the word maṣṇāra. Padaratnāvalī: ‘At the conclusion of a particular observance (vrata) called Maṣṇāra.’ All of them state that (alternatively) Maṣṇāra is the name of a holy place.

Footnote [5]:
The elephants are of the three types: bhadra, mandra and mṛga.

Footnote [6]:
Gf. Ait. Br. 8.23.3. Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa regards these elephant-types as belonging to the region of the Himalayas, Vindhya and Sahyādri respectively.

devanagari: भरतस्य महत् कर्म न पूर्वे नापरे नृपाः । नैवापुर्नैव प्राप्स्यन्ति बाहुभ्यां त्रिदिवं यथा ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: bharatasya mahat karma na pūrve nāpare nṛpāḥ naivāpurnaiva prāpsyanti bāhubhyāṃ tridivaṃ yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[imperfect active third plural], [aorist active third plural]

translation: No king of the past could accomplish the great eminence of Bharata. Nor can kings of the future do so any more than one can reach the celestial world by hands.

devanagari: किरातहूणान् यवनान् अंध्रान् कंकान् खगान् शकान् । अब्रह्मण्यान् नृपांश्चाहन् म्लेच्छान् दिग्विजयेऽखिलान् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: kirātahūṇān yavanān aṃdhrān kaṃkān khagān śakān abrahmaṇyān nṛpāṃścāhan mlecchān digvijaye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: During his expedition for the conquest of the world, he defeated the tribes Kirātas, Hūṇas, Yavanas, Āndhras, Kaṅkas, Khaśas and Śakas and killed the Mleccha kings who were inimical to Brahmins or Vedas.

devanagari: जित्वा पुरासुरा देवान् ये रसौकांसि भेजिरे । देवस्त्रियो रसां नीताः प्राणिभिः पुनराहरत् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: jitvā purāsurā devān ye rasaukāṃsi bhejire devastriyo rasāṃ nītāḥ prāṇibhiḥ punarāharat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ nī class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ nī class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ nī class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ nī class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ nī class 1 verb]

translation: [7] By sending messengers, he restored to the gods, the celestial damsels who were formerly abducted to Rasātala by Asuras, after vanquishing the gods and occupying various parts of that region.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Vanquishing the Asuras, he repatriated gods to their respective regions and restored to their husbands the celestial damsels who were carried away by demons celled Paṇis—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: सर्वान् कामान् दुदुहतुः प्रजानां तस्य रोदसी । समास्त्रिणवसाहस्रीः दिक्षु चक्रमवर्तयत् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvān kāmān duduhatuḥ prajānāṃ tasya rodasī samāstriṇavasāhasrīḥ dikṣu cakramavartayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural]
[nominative single from √ vṛt ], [vocative single from √ vṛt ], [accusative single from √ vṛt ]

translation: While he ruled, both heaven and earth supplied desires and needs of his subjects. His sovereignty extended all over the world for thrice nine thousand (27000) years.

devanagari: स सम्राड् लोकपालाख्यं ऐश्वर्यं अधिराट् श्रियम् । चक्रं चास्खलितं प्राणान् मृन्मृषेत्युपरराम ह ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa samrāḍ lokapālākhyaṃ aiśvaryaṃ adhirāṭ śriyam cakraṃ cāskhalitaṃ prāṇān mṛnmṛṣetyupararāma ha
translation: The tradition goes that the emperor realized that his sovereignty the fame of which had spread over all the worlds, his imperial glory and affluence, his unchallenged authority and implicit obedience to commands, nay even his life and sense-organs were unreal and so he renounced the world.

devanagari: तस्यासन् नृप वैदर्भ्यः पत्‍न्यस्तिस्रः सुसम्मताः । जघ्नुस्त्यागभयात् पुत्रान् नानुरूपा इतीरिते ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāsan nṛpa vaidarbhyaḥ pat‍nyastisraḥ susammatāḥ jaghnustyāgabhayāt putrān nānurūpā itīrite
translation: He had three queens all from the state of Vidarbha. When he remarked that his sons did not look like him, they, being afraid’ of being abandoned by him (suspecting their character), killed them all.

devanagari: तस्यैवं वितथे वंशे तदर्थं यजतः सुतम् । मरुत्स्तोमेन मरुतो भरद्वाजमुपाददुः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyaivaṃ vitathe vaṃśe tadarthaṃ yajataḥ sutam marutstomena maruto bharadvājamupādaduḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single], [imperative passive first single]

translation: When his family line was (in the danger of being) extinct, the Marut gods gave him Bharadvāja as a son, when the emperor was propitiating them with sacrifice Marutstoma (forgetting a son).

devanagari: अन्तर्वत्‍न्यां भ्रातृपत्‍न्यां मैथुनाय बृहस्पतिः । प्रवृत्तो वारितो गर्भं शप्त्वा वीर्यमुपासृजत् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: antarvat‍nyāṃ bhrātṛpat‍nyāṃ maithunāya bṛhaspatiḥ pravṛtto vārito garbhaṃ śaptvā vīryamupāsṛjat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ vīr class 10 verb]

translation: When Mamatā, the wife of his brother (Utathya), was pregnant, Bṛhaspati attempted to have a sexual intercourse with her, and was prevented (by the child in the womb with strong loud protests). He (Bṛhaspati) cursed the child in the womb (to become blind). He discharged per force his semen (in her), but the blind child in the womb prevented its entry by his foot. The seed that fell out on the ground assumed the form of a child.

devanagari: तं त्यक्तुकामां ममतां भर्तुत्यागविशंकिताम् । नामनिर्वाचनं तस्य श्लोकमेनं सुरा जगुः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tyaktukāmāṃ mamatāṃ bhartutyāgaviśaṃkitām nāmanirvācanaṃ tasya ślokamenaṃ surā jaguḥ
translation: To Mamatā who wanted to give away that child as she was terribly afraid of being abandoned by her husband, gods spoke the following verse which contains the etymology of the name of the child (which is nothing but the quarrel between Bṛhaspati and Mamatā each throwing the responsibility of bringing up that child on the other).

devanagari: मूढे भर द्वाजं इमं भर द्वाजं बृहस्पते । यातौ यदुक्त्वा पितरौ भरद्वाजस्ततस्त्वयम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: mūḍhe bhara dvājaṃ imaṃ bhara dvājaṃ bṛhaspate yātau yaduktvā pitarau bharadvājastatastvayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ muh class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ muh class 4 verb], [locative single from √ muh class 4 verb]
[nominative dual from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ muh class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ muh class 4 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ yā class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ yā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
(SṚ. records this controversy between Mamatā and Bṛhaspati as follows):
(Said Bṛhaspati:) “Oh foolish woman! Nourish (this child). (If you say that you are afraid of your husband that is baseless as) this child is born of us two brothers (by me through the Kṣetra viz. the wife of my brother)!” (Replied Mamatā) “Bṛhaspati! You nourish this child because he is born of us two (when you raped me)”, Saying these words (bhara dvājam) both the parents went away (abandoning the child). Hence he is called Bhara-dvāja.

devanagari: चोद्यमाना सुरैरेवं मत्वा वितथमात्मजम् । व्यसृजन् मरुतोऽबिभ्रन् दत्तोऽयं वितथेऽन्वये ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: codyamānā surairevaṃ matvā vitathamātmajam vyasṛjan maruto
translation:
Though urged by gods (to bring up the child), they thought him as a useless son and abandoned him. The Maruts nourished the child and it was given to Bharata when his family was going to be extinct. (Vitatha should be regarded as the name of Bharadvāja, after his adoption by Bharata).
